title: pelea elongata
intro: Pelea elongata, also known as the Long-stalked Pelea, is a flowering plant native to the tropical forests of Hawaii and Southeast Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities, which can be used to treat a variety of health issues, such as wounds and skin irritations. The health benefits of Pelea elongata include reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting immune function. Herbal preparations of Pelea elongata are typically made by infusing the leaves and flowers in hot water to create a tea or by using them in a topical cream or ointment to apply directly to affected areas.
